title: Banque Commerciale pour l'Europe du Nord – Eurobank

infobox.key: Banque Commerciale pour l’Europe du Nord
infobox.value: (French pronunciation: [[bɑ̃k kɔmɛʁsjal puʁ løʁɔp dy nɔʁ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), **BCEN**) or **Banque Commerciale pour l'Europe du Nord – Eurobank** (**BCEN-Eurobank**) was a [Soviet](/wiki/Soviet "Soviet")-controlled bank in Paris,[[1]](#cite_note-goldman-1) founded in 1921 by wealthy Russian emigres and supported by the [Council of People's Commissars](/wiki/Council_of_People%27s_Commissars "Council of People's Commissars") (SovNarKom) ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Совнарком) through [Leonid Krasin](/wiki/Leonid_Krasin "Leonid Krasin") who, in 1925, sold their stakes to the Soviet Union.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It maintained correspondent accounts with Western banks to secure lines of credit for facilitating Soviet imports into that country in which the correspondent account is located. Also, these correspondent accounts performed foreign currency exchange for the Kremlin.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Leonid Georgievich Kucheruk ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Леонид Георгиевич Кучерук; born [Agronomic](https://ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D0%90%D0%B3%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%BE%D0%BC%D0%B8%D1%87%D0%BD%D0%BE%D0%B5_(%D0%92%D0%B8%D0%BD%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%86%D0%BA%D0%B0%D1%8F_%D0%BE%D0%B1%D0%BB%D0%B0%D1%81%D1%82%D1%8C) "ru:Агрономичное (Винницкая область)"), [Vinnytsia Raion](/wiki/Vinnytsia_Raion "Vinnytsia Raion"), [Vinnytsia Oblast](/wiki/Vinnytsia_Oblast "Vinnytsia Oblast"), [Ukrainian SSR](/wiki/Ukrainian_SSR "Ukrainian SSR"), [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union") died 22 December 1996 [Minsk](/wiki/Minsk "Minsk"), [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus")) managed one of the financial links between the Soviet Union and [French Communist Party](/wiki/French_Communist_Party "French Communist Party") (PCF). He had fought in the [Soviet War in Afghanistan](/wiki/Soviet_War_in_Afghanistan "Soviet War in Afghanistan"), worked for the magazine *[Sovetsky Soyuz](/wiki/Sovetsky_Soyuz "Sovetsky Soyuz")* in Mexico as his cover for his KGB activities, and was a [KGB](/wiki/KGB "KGB") Colonel when died of wounds inflicted during torture. His close associate in Mexico was the intelligence officer and photographer also with *Sovetsky Soyuz* Yuri Korolev ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Юрий Королев) who died on 8 November 1994 from injuries inflicted during torture. On 27 January 1997, Vadim Osipovich Biryukov ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Вадим Осипович Бирюков), who was a KGB officer, was a colleague of both Korolev and Kucheruk, and was a deputy director general of the magazine *Business People* ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): «Деловые люди»), died from injuries inflicted during torture.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Following the February and March 2022 sanctions issued against VTB and its subsidiaries which include the former daughter banks of the Soviet Union's State Bank [Gosbank](/wiki/Gosbank "Gosbank") and later the [Central Bank of Russia](/wiki/Central_Bank_of_Russia "Central Bank of Russia"), EWUB in Luxembourg has become the principal Russian overseas bank in Europe after [Gazprombank](/wiki/Gazprombank "Gazprombank")'s liquidation.[[18]](#cite_note-Delano28022022-19) As of the end of March 2022, the [Vladimir Yevtushenkov](/wiki/Vladimir_Yevtushenkov "Vladimir Yevtushenkov") ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Владимир Петрович Евтушенко) controlled Sistema and its subsidiary East-West United Bank (EWUB) in Luxembourg have not been sanctioned due to [Russian interference in Ukraine](/wiki/Russo-Ukrainian_War "Russo-Ukrainian War").[[18]](#cite_note-Delano28022022-19)[[19]](#cite_note-20)
The piratization of Russia: Russian reform goes awry. Marshall I. Goldman.

Сухотина, Инна (Sukhotina, Inna) (10 November 2003). ["Сколько стоит приданое "дочек" Банка России?"](https://web.archive.org/web/20031129165942/https://rg.ru/2003/11/10/banki.html) [How much is the dowry of the "daughters" of the Bank of Russia?]. *[«Российская газета»](/wiki/Rossiyskaya_Gazeta "Rossiyskaya Gazeta") (Rossiyskaya Gazeta)* (in Russian). Archived from [the original](https://rg.ru/2003/11/10/banki.html) on 29 November 2003. Retrieved 16 October 2023.`{{cite news}}`: CS1 maint: multiple names: authors list ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_multiple_names:_authors_list "Category:CS1 maint: multiple names: authors list"))

["Intelligence Report Soviet-owned Banks in the West"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160304080756/http://www.foia.cia.gov/sites/default/files/document_conversions/89801/DOC_0000233857.pdf) (PDF). Archived from [the original](https://www.foia.cia.gov/sites/default/files/document_conversions/89801/DOC_0000233857.pdf) (PDF) on 2016-03-04. Retrieved 2015-04-24.

["Виктор Константинович Якунин: Нашим возможностям соответствовал, Ost-West Handelsbank"](https://web.archive.org/web/20210813011601/http://letopis.org/static/org/images/directs/6/49b13b66-3777-11ea-9e5e-001be2a7c7fd.pdf) [Victor Konstantinovich Yakunin: Our capabilities matched, Ost-West Handelsbank] (PDF). *letopis.org* (in Russian). pp. 2–3 (pages 459–460). Archived from [the original](http://letopis.org/static/org/images/directs/6/49b13b66-3777-11ea-9e5e-001be2a7c7fd.pdf) (PDF) on 13 August 2021. Retrieved 12 August 2021.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former bank in France

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b7/Paris_Haussmann_81.jpg/250px-Paris_Haussmann_81.jpg)](/wiki/File:Paris_Haussmann_81.jpg)

Former head office of BCEN-Eurobank at 79-81, boulevard Haussmann in Paris

**Banque Commerciale pour l’Europe du Nord** (French pronunciation: [[bɑ̃k kɔmɛʁsjal puʁ løʁɔp dy nɔʁ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), **BCEN**) or **Banque Commerciale pour l'Europe du Nord – Eurobank** (**BCEN-Eurobank**) was a [Soviet](/wiki/Soviet "Soviet")-controlled bank in Paris,[[1]](#cite_note-goldman-1) founded in 1921 by wealthy Russian emigres and supported by the [Council of People's Commissars](/wiki/Council_of_People%27s_Commissars "Council of People's Commissars") (SovNarKom) ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Совнарком) through [Leonid Krasin](/wiki/Leonid_Krasin "Leonid Krasin") who, in 1925, sold their stakes to the Soviet Union.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It maintained correspondent accounts with Western banks to secure lines of credit for facilitating Soviet imports into that country in which the correspondent account is located. Also, these correspondent accounts performed foreign currency exchange for the Kremlin.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banque_Commerciale_pour_l%27Europe_du_Nord_%E2%80%93_Eurobank&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

During the 1920s and 1930s, Soviet and numerous foreign sources referred to this bank as the Aero Bank ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): «Аеробанк») or Airbank in Paris ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): «Эйробанк» в Париже) and not the Aero-Bank SA which was a branch of the German bank *Aerobank*, which was known as the [Bank der Deutschen Luftfahrt AG (BDL)](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bank_der_Deutschen_Luftfahrt "de:Bank der Deutschen Luftfahrt"), and established in Paris by Nazi Germany during its occupation.[[4]](#cite_note-AlibegovSovietBanking-4)

The [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union") used the bank to [manage Spain's gold reserves](/wiki/Moscow_gold "Moscow gold") during the [Spanish Civil War](/wiki/Spanish_Civil_War "Spanish Civil War").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

After the fall of the Soviet Union, many persons involved with BCEN-Eurobank became leaders in Russian economy, banking, and finance.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The 1993 established Moscow bank "[Evrofinance](/wiki/Evrofinance_Mosnarbank "Evrofinance Mosnarbank")" was a subsidiary of Eurobank.[[7]](#cite_note-PonomaryovBioLetopis-7)

During the 1990s, BCEN – Eurobank was involved in the [looting of Russia](/wiki/FIMACO "FIMACO").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 2005, [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia")'s [Vneshtorgbank](/wiki/VTB_Bank "VTB Bank") became the major shareholder. Later, Vneshtorgbank changed its name to [VTB Bank](/wiki/VTB_Bank "VTB Bank"), and BCEN-EUROBANK name was changed to **VTB Bank (France) SA**.

## Operations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banque_Commerciale_pour_l%27Europe_du_Nord_%E2%80%93_Eurobank&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Operations")]

Eurobank participated in foreign currency exchange and [Eurocurrency](/wiki/Eurocurrency "Eurocurrency") markets and was a leader in [Eurodollar](/wiki/Eurodollar "Eurodollar") lending. In December 1973, Eurobank and [Credit Lyonnais](/wiki/Credit_Lyonnais "Credit Lyonnais") formed the *Societe pour la Promotion Europeenne du Leasing* (Promolease) based in Paris to promote and finance leasing operations for Soviet heavy construction equipment, Soviet snowplows, Soviet machine tools, Soviet [Lada](/wiki/Lada "Lada") vehicles, and Czechoslovak [Škoda Auto](/wiki/%C5%A0koda_Auto "Škoda Auto") vehicles.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

The Soviet Union supplied funds through Eurobank to the [French Communist Party](/wiki/French_Communist_Party "French Communist Party") (PCF).[[10]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBelton2020543_see_note_26-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[a]](#cite_note-16)

The bank had its offices at 77, 79, and 81, boulevard Haussmann in Paris.[[16]](#cite_note-17)

## Other associated banks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banque_Commerciale_pour_l%27Europe_du_Nord_%E2%80%93_Eurobank&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Other associated banks")]

[VTB](/wiki/VTB "VTB") has the western subsidiaries of the [Central Bank of Russia](/wiki/Central_Bank_of_Russia "Central Bank of Russia") and, before the dissolution of the Soviet Union, the daughters of the [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union")'s [Gosbank](/wiki/Gosbank "Gosbank") which were the Banque commerciale pour l'Europe du Nord or BCEN-Eurobank in Paris, [Moscow Narodny Bank](/wiki/Moscow_Narodny_Bank "Moscow Narodny Bank") in [London](/wiki/London "London"), [Ost-West Handelsbank](/wiki/Ost-West_Handelsbank "Ost-West Handelsbank") in [Frankfurt](/wiki/Frankfurt "Frankfurt"), [Donau Bank](/wiki/Donau_Bank "Donau Bank") in [Vienna](/wiki/Vienna "Vienna"), and [East-West United Bank](/wiki/East-West_United_Bank "East-West United Bank") in [Luxembourg](/wiki/Luxembourg "Luxembourg").[[17]](#cite_note-18)[[b]](#cite_note-21)

Eurobank was associated with Eurogrefi (Paris), RTD France (Paris), Evrofinance (Moscow) and others.[[20]](#cite_note-MN12082003-22)

## Key people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banque_Commerciale_pour_l%27Europe_du_Nord_%E2%80%93_Eurobank&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Key people")]

* [Thomas Alibegov](/wiki/Thomas_Alibegov "Thomas Alibegov"), Director General (1982–1987)[[4]](#cite_note-AlibegovSovietBanking-4)[[21]](#cite_note-23)
* [Yury Ponomaryov](/wiki/Yury_Ponomaryov_(banker) "Yury Ponomaryov (banker)"), CEO, chairman of the Board of Directors (1988–1998)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banque_Commerciale_pour_l%27Europe_du_Nord_%E2%80%93_Eurobank&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Foreign trade of the Soviet Union](/wiki/Foreign_trade_of_the_Soviet_Union "Foreign trade of the Soviet Union")
* [Banking in the Soviet Union](/wiki/Banking_in_the_Soviet_Union "Banking in the Soviet Union")
* [List of banks in France](/wiki/List_of_banks_in_France "List of banks in France")

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banque_Commerciale_pour_l%27Europe_du_Nord_%E2%80%93_Eurobank&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-16)** Leonid Georgievich Kucheruk ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Леонид Георгиевич Кучерук; born [Agronomic](https://ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D0%90%D0%B3%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%BE%D0%BC%D0%B8%D1%87%D0%BD%D0%BE%D0%B5_(%D0%92%D0%B8%D0%BD%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%86%D0%BA%D0%B0%D1%8F_%D0%BE%D0%B1%D0%BB%D0%B0%D1%81%D1%82%D1%8C) "ru:Агрономичное (Винницкая область)"), [Vinnytsia Raion](/wiki/Vinnytsia_Raion "Vinnytsia Raion"), [Vinnytsia Oblast](/wiki/Vinnytsia_Oblast "Vinnytsia Oblast"), [Ukrainian SSR](/wiki/Ukrainian_SSR "Ukrainian SSR"), [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union") died 22 December 1996 [Minsk](/wiki/Minsk "Minsk"), [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus")) managed one of the financial links between the Soviet Union and [French Communist Party](/wiki/French_Communist_Party "French Communist Party") (PCF). He had fought in the [Soviet War in Afghanistan](/wiki/Soviet_War_in_Afghanistan "Soviet War in Afghanistan"), worked for the magazine *[Sovetsky Soyuz](/wiki/Sovetsky_Soyuz "Sovetsky Soyuz")* in Mexico as his cover for his KGB activities, and was a [KGB](/wiki/KGB "KGB") Colonel when died of wounds inflicted during torture. His close associate in Mexico was the intelligence officer and photographer also with *Sovetsky Soyuz* Yuri Korolev ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Юрий Королев) who died on 8 November 1994 from injuries inflicted during torture. On 27 January 1997, Vadim Osipovich Biryukov ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Вадим Осипович Бирюков), who was a KGB officer, was a colleague of both Korolev and Kucheruk, and was a deputy director general of the magazine *Business People* ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): «Деловые люди»), died from injuries inflicted during torture.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)
2. **[^](#cite_ref-21)** Following the February and March 2022 sanctions issued against VTB and its subsidiaries which include the former daughter banks of the Soviet Union's State Bank [Gosbank](/wiki/Gosbank "Gosbank") and later the [Central Bank of Russia](/wiki/Central_Bank_of_Russia "Central Bank of Russia"), EWUB in Luxembourg has become the principal Russian overseas bank in Europe after [Gazprombank](/wiki/Gazprombank "Gazprombank")'s liquidation.[[18]](#cite_note-Delano28022022-19) As of the end of March 2022, the [Vladimir Yevtushenkov](/wiki/Vladimir_Yevtushenkov "Vladimir Yevtushenkov") ([Russian](/wiki/Russian_language "Russian language"): Владимир Петрович Евтушенко) controlled Sistema and its subsidiary East-West United Bank (EWUB) in Luxembourg have not been sanctioned due to [Russian interference in Ukraine](/wiki/Russo-Ukrainian_War "Russo-Ukrainian War").[[18]](#cite_note-Delano28022022-19)[[19]](#cite_note-20)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Banque_Commerciale_pour_l%27Europe_du_Nord_%E2%80%93_Eurobank&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: References")]

Archived from [the original](https://rg.ru/2003/11/10/banki.html) on 29 November 2003. Retrieved 16 October 2023.`{{cite news}}`: CS1 maint: multiple names: authors list ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_multiple_names:_authors_list "Category:CS1 maint: multiple names: authors list"))
